Anji Microelectronics Technology Shanghai 2024 Profit Up 33%, Revenue Rises 48%
data.description:
Anji Microelectronics Technology Shanghai posted 2024 net profit attributable to shareholders of 533.6 million yuan, up 33% from 402.7 million yuan the previous year.

Earnings per share rose to 4.14 yuan from 3.14 yuan, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The semiconductor materials maker's revenue rose 48% year over year to 1.84 billion yuan from 1.24 billion yuan.

Anji Microelectronics Technology Shanghai declared a dividend of 0.45 yuan per share.

Shares of the company declined 2% in recent trade.

China Appoints New Trade Negotiator Amid U.S. Tariff War
data.description:
China has appointed a new top trade negotiator amid the tariff war with the U.S.

Li Chenggang was named international trade representative and vice commerce minister, according to a statement Wednesday from the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Security.

Li replaces Vice Commerce Minister Wang Shouwen as China's lead trade negotiator and is expected to play a central role in talks with the Trump administration, which on Tuesday signaled it remains open to reaching a deal with Beijing.

Before the latest appointment, Li served as China's ambassador to the World Trade Organization since 2021, with decades of experience in global trade. He holds a law degree from Peking University and a master's in law and economics from the University of Hamburg in Germany.

The ministry also on Wednesday said the government appointed Wang Zhizhong as the new head of the National Immigration Administration.

Write to Singapore Editors at singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Zuari Agro Chemicals Pays INR500 Million for Early Redemption of Debentures
data.description:
Zuari Agro Chemicals , has paid 500 million Indian rupees towards early redemption of two tranches of non-convertible debentures, according to a Tuesday filing with the Indian stock exchanges.

The company has executed early redemption for 500 senior, secured, unrated, unlisted, redeemable, non-convertible debentures of 1 million rupees each.

data.title: Gold Road Starts Drilling at Western Australia Project; Shares Rise 3%
data.description:
Gold Road Resources started a AU$10.4 million drilling program at its Gilmour project in Western Australia, according to a Wednesday filing with the Australian bourse.

The 30,800-meter campaign includes both infill and extensional drilling and will assess the underground potential of the project, the filing said.

Insufficient exploration to date makes it uncertain if further drilling will define a mineral resource, the filing added.

The program is expected to be completed by the September quarter, the company said.

Shares of the company rose 3% in recent Wednesday trade.

UPM-Kymmene's Plywood Unit Fails to Reach Labor Deal with Industrial Union
data.description:
Negotiations between UPM-Kymmene's UPM Plywood and the Industrial Union failed after the union rejected a new proposal by the mediator on Tuesday, according to a same-day release.

The union will continue to strike at the UPM Plywood mills in Finland, where production remains halted. The settlement process will continue as soon as conciliator Jukka Ahtela deems it appropriate.

The Finnish forest and paper products company accepted Ahtela's new proposal.

data.title: JieJie Microelectronics' 2024 Attributable Profit Soars 116%; Shares Fall 3%
data.description:
Jiangsu JieJie Microelectronics' attributable profit surged 116% to 473 million yuan in 2024 from 219.1 million yuan in 2023, according to an annual report filed Wednesday with the Shenzhen bourse.

Earnings per share at the electronic component manufacturer increased 114% to 0.60 yuan from 0.28 yuan in the previous year.

Operating income grew 35% to 2.84 billion yuan from 2.11 billion yuan a year earlier.

JieJie Microelectronics' shares fell 3% during Wednesday's afternoon trading.

data.title: AGII Officially Launches AI-Powered Web3 App, Ushering in a New Era of Decentralized Intelligence
data.description:
Subtitle: The launch of AGII's innovative app delivers intelligent tools for automation, smart contracts, and secure Web3 integration.

Seattle, Washington--(Newsfile Corp. - April 16, 2025) - AGII, the AI-powered Web3 platform, has officially launched its much-anticipated application, marking a major leap forward in decentralized technology. Designed to streamline blockchain interactions and optimize smart contract deployment, the AGII App integrates cutting-edge artificial intelligence with Web3 functionality-ushering in a new era of intelligent automation across decentralized ecosystems.

Smarter blockchain tools powered by advanced AI for next-gen Web3 solutions.

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/8833/248669_93d9de6eb4ef7866_001full.jpg

The AGII App offers a comprehensive suite of features including real-time fraud detection, predictive automation, and adaptive contract execution-built to enhance blockchain scalability and performance. With a user-friendly interface and robust backend architecture, developers, enterprises, and decentralized application (dApp) creators can now manage complex workflows with increased efficiency and trust.

By combining scalable AI models with on-chain infrastructure, the AGII App delivers intelligent, autonomous solutions that learn and adapt to real-time blockchain activity. This level of automation significantly reduces latency and enhances decision-making across decentralized networks, making it a cornerstone for next-gen Web3 platforms.

The official release reinforces AGII's commitment to innovation and leadership in the AI-Web3 sector. With continued development on cross-chain capabilities and additional tools planned in future updates, AGII is setting new standards for secure, scalable, and intelligent blockchain solutions.

About AGII

AGII is a next-generation AI-powered Web3 platform focused on enhancing blockchain functionality through automation, scalability, and secure smart contract management. Its innovative solutions are built to empower developers, enterprises, and decentralized networks with real-time, intelligent tools for the evolving digital landscape.

data.title: Rupee finds breathing room after sharpest two-week swings since 2023
data.description:
The Indian rupee extended gains on Wednesday as global markets steadied after a fortnight of U.S. tariff-driven frenzy that had sent the currency's two-week volatility to its highest since January 2023.

The rupee rose 0.2% to 85.61 as of 10:25 a.m. IST, aided by continued weakness in the dollar which has also contributed to interest in taking long bets on the local unit.

The rupee has swung between 84.9575 and 86.71 over the past two weeks, driven largely by tariff-related developments. The dollar-rupee pair's 2-week realised volatility (INR2WVCC=R) is at nearly 6%, highest since January 2023.

The pronounced moves have come amid global market dislocations triggered by tariff moves and a relatively hands-off approach by the Indian central bank in managing the currency this year.

Heightened volatility is "good in some ways as it (USD/INR) is not being controlled as closely but people are also not used to it, so there is an adjustment period," a treasury official at a multi-national firm said.

On the day, the dollar was down 0.3% against major peers while Asian currencies were trading mixed. The Chinese yuan was slightly lower amid worries over the worsening trade relationship between the world's two largest economies.

President Donald Trump has ordered a probe into potential new tariffs on all U.S. critical minerals imports while Beijing has reportedly ordered airlines to suspend deliveries of Boeing aircraft.

While trade worries will remain front and centre for currency markets, falling inflation locally may give India's central bank more room to support growth via easing of monetary policy.

"We have a call for a further 50 bps of rate cuts by the RBI with risks titled towards more rather than less cuts," MUFG Bank said in a note.

data.title: Factors to watch on April 16
data.description:
Britain's FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 index is seen opening lower on Wednesday, with futures down 0.42%.

* RIO TINTO: Rio Tinto , reported its lowest first-quarter iron ore shipments since 2019 and warned that more weather disruptions could lead to a 2025 forecast miss, after cyclones impacted the miner's Pilbara operations.

* PLAYTECH: British gambling technology firm Playtech is set to name former DAZN executive John Gleasure as its next chairman, Sky News reported.

* POWER GRID: Britain's power grid could experience its lowest ever electricity demand this summer as renewables generation and cheap power imports from Europe keep the system well supplied, the National Energy System Operator said.

* OIL: Oil prices edged lower as shifting U.S. tariff policies fuelled uncertainty.

* GOLD: Gold prices hit an all-time peak as a weaker dollar, escalating trade tensions and concerns over global economic growth increased demand for the safe-haven bullion.

* METALS: London copper prices fell on persistent global economic uncertainty triggered by tariffs, while investors awaited stimulus from China.

* The UK blue-chip index CURRENCYCOM:UK100 closed up 1.4% on Tuesday, led by financial and defence stocks, after U.S. President Donald Trump signalled possible exemptions on auto-related levies and investors assessed job market data for monetary policy insights.
